words.definitionEn: in or to a foreign country
words.exampleEn: to go/travel/live/study abroad

words.definitionEn: an event in which people compete with each other to find out who is the best at something
words.exampleEn: a design/an art/a talent competition

words.definitionEn: to say that you are annoyed, unhappy or not satisfied about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She never complains, but she's obviously exhausted.

words.definitionEn: in every way possible; in every part
words.exampleEn: It was completely different from how I had imagined it.

words.definitionEn: fully grown or developed
words.exampleEn: preparing young people for adult life

words.definitionEn: the state that something is in
words.exampleEn: in… condition, to be in pristine/excellent/perfect condition

words.definitionEn: a large official meeting, usually lasting for a few days, at which people with the same work or interests come together to discuss their views
words.exampleEn: The hotel is used for exhibitions, conferences and social events.

words.definitionEn: to join together two or more things; to be joined together
words.exampleEn: connect A and B, The towns are connected by train and bus services.

words.definitionEn: (of two or more things or people) having a link between them
words.exampleEn: We do not believe that the incidents are connected.

words.definitionEn: to think about something carefully, especially in order to make a decision
words.exampleEn: I'd like some time to consider.

words.definitionEn: a thing that helps you to be better or more successful than other people
words.exampleEn: a distinct/significant/huge advantage

words.definitionEn: an unusual, exciting or dangerous experience, journey or series of events
words.exampleEn: her adventures travelling in Africa

words.definitionEn: if something contains something else, it has that thing inside it or as part of it
words.exampleEn: This drink doesn't contain any alcohol.

words.definitionEn: the situation in which something happens and that helps you to understand it
words.exampleEn: Such databases are being used in a wide range of contexts.

words.definitionEn: one of the large land masses of the earth such as Europe, Asia or Africa
words.exampleEn: the continent of Africa

words.definitionEn: to tell the public about a product or a service in order to encourage people to buy or to use it
words.exampleEn: If you want to attract customers you need to advertise.

words.definitionEn: to keep existing or happening without stopping
words.exampleEn: If the current trend continues, that number will increase 165 per cent by 2050.

words.definitionEn: a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service
words.exampleEn: a newspaper/television advertisement

words.definitionEn: the power to make decisions about how a country, an area, an organization, etc. is run
words.exampleEn: control of somebody/something, The party expects to gain control of the council in the next election.

words.definitionEn: to have power over a person, company, country, etc. so that you are able to decide what they must do or how it is run
words.exampleEn: By the age of 21 he controlled the company.

words.definitionEn: the activity and industry of advertising things to people on television, in newspapers, on the internet, etc.
words.exampleEn: Cigarette advertising has been banned.

words.definitionEn: a person who cooks food or whose job is cooking
words.exampleEn: John is a very good cook (= he cooks well).

words.definitionEn: a large piece of equipment for cooking food, containing an oven and gas or electric rings on top
words.exampleEn: a gas cooker

words.definitionEn: a thing that is made to be the same as something else, especially a document or a work of art
words.exampleEn: The thieves replaced the original painting with a copy.

words.definitionEn: to make something that is exactly like something else
words.exampleEn: copy something, He taught himself by copying paintings in the Louvre.

words.definitionEn: a part of something where two or more sides, lines or edges join
words.exampleEn: the four corners of a square

words.definitionEn: in a way that is accurate or true, without any mistakes
words.exampleEn: Have you spelled it correctly?

words.definitionEn: to say numbers in the correct order
words.exampleEn: Billy can't count yet.

words.definitionEn: two people or things
words.exampleEn: I saw a couple of men get out.

words.definitionEn: to produce a change in somebody/something
words.exampleEn: How will these changes affect us?

words.definitionEn: to place something over or in front of something in order to hide, protect or decorate it
words.exampleEn: cover something with something, Cover the chicken loosely with foil.

words.definitionEn: not sensible; stupid
words.exampleEn: Are you crazy? We could get killed doing that.

words.definitionEn: involving the use of skill and the imagination to produce something new or a work of art
words.exampleEn: a course on creative writing (= writing stories, plays and poems)

words.definitionEn: an arrangement that you make, with a shop for example, to pay later for something you buy
words.exampleEn: to get credit

words.definitionEn: activities that involve breaking the law
words.exampleEn: This month's figures show an increase in violent crime.

words.definitionEn: a person who commits a crime
words.exampleEn: a convicted criminal

words.definitionEn: a mark or an object formed by two lines crossing each other (X or +); the mark (X) is often used on paper to show something
words.exampleEn: I've put a cross on the map to show where the hotel is.

words.definitionEn: to go across; to pass or stretch from one side to the other
words.exampleEn: As soon as traffic slowed down enough to safely cross, I started walking.

words.definitionEn: a large number of people gathered together in a public place, for example in the streets or at a sports game
words.exampleEn: He pushed his way through the crowd.

words.definitionEn: having a lot of people or too many people
words.exampleEn: We made our way through the crowded streets.

words.definitionEn: later in time; afterwards
words.exampleEn: That was in 1996. Soon after, I heard that he'd died.

words.definitionEn: to produce tears from your eyes because you are unhappy or hurt
words.exampleEn: It's all right. Don't cry.

words.definitionEn: at a time later than something; when something has finished
words.exampleEn: I'll call you after I've spoken to them.

words.definitionEn: a piece of furniture with doors and shelves used for storing dishes, food, clothes, etc.
words.exampleEn: kitchen cupboards

words.definitionEn: having a lot of curls or a curved shape
words.exampleEn: long curly hair

words.definitionEn: a bicycle or motorcycle
words.exampleEn: He was riding his cycle home when he was hit by a lorry.

words.definitionEn: to ride a bicycle; to travel by bicycle
words.exampleEn: measures to encourage more people to cycle

words.definitionEn: happening, done or produced every day
words.exampleEn: Many people still read a daily newspaper.

words.definitionEn: the possibility of something happening that will injure, harm or kill somebody, or damage or destroy something
words.exampleEn: Danger! Keep Out!

words.definitionEn: the lack of light in a place, especially because it is night
words.exampleEn: Are the children afraid of the dark?

words.definitionEn: facts or information, especially when examined and used to find out things or to make decisions
words.exampleEn: We collected publicly available data over a 10-day period.

words.definitionEn: no longer alive
words.exampleEn: My mother's dead; she died in 2017.

words.definitionEn: to give cards to each player in a game of cards
words.exampleEn: Whose turn is it to deal?

words.definitionEn: opposing or disagreeing with somebody/something
words.exampleEn: the fight against terrorism

words.definitionEn: used in expressions that show that you are surprised, upset, annoyed or worried
words.exampleEn: Oh dear! I think I've lost my purse!

words.definitionEn: the fact of somebody dying or being killed
words.exampleEn: the anniversary of his wife’s death

words.definitionEn: a choice or judgement that you make after thinking and talking about what is the best thing to do
words.exampleEn: I think I've made the right decision.

words.definitionEn: having a large distance from the top or surface to the bottom
words.exampleEn: a deep hole/well/river

words.definitionEn: a way of emphasizing that something is true and that there is no doubt about it
words.exampleEn: I definitely remember sending the letter.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring temperature
words.exampleEn: at… degrees, Water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit (32°F) or zero/nought degrees Celsius (0°C).

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to take care of people’s teeth
words.exampleEn: to see/visit/consult your dentist

words.definitionEn: a section of a large organization such as a government, business, university, etc.
words.exampleEn: department of something, the Department of Health

words.definitionEn: according to
words.exampleEn: Starting salary varies from £26 000 to £30 500, depending on experience.

words.definitionEn: a large area of land that has very little water and very few plants growing on it. Many deserts are covered by sand.
words.exampleEn: the Sahara Desert

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to decide how things such as clothes, furniture, tools, etc. will look or work by making drawings, plans or patterns
words.exampleEn: one of the country's top fashion designers

words.definitionEn: to damage something so badly that it no longer exists, works, etc.
words.exampleEn: The building was completely destroyed by fire.

words.definitionEn: a person, especially a police officer, whose job is to investigate crimes and catch criminals
words.exampleEn: a police/homicide/undercover detective

words.definitionEn: to gradually grow or become bigger, more advanced, stronger, etc.; to make something do this
words.exampleEn: From birth to age 5, a child's brain develops rapidly.

words.definitionEn: an object or a piece of equipment that has been designed to do a particular job
words.exampleEn: a tracking/recording/listening device

words.definitionEn: a book in which you can write down the experiences you have each day, your private thoughts, etc.
words.exampleEn: Do you keep a diary (= write one regularly)?

words.definitionEn: used to express surprise, pleasure or sympathy, or when you disagree with somebody
words.exampleEn: Ah, there you are!

words.definitionEn: in a different way from somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Boys and girls may behave differently.

words.definitionEn: using a system of receiving and sending information as a series of the numbers one and zero, showing that an electronic signal is there or is not there
words.exampleEn: a digital camera

words.definitionEn: going in the straightest line between two places without stopping or changing direction
words.exampleEn: the most direct route

words.definitionEn: the general position a person or thing moves or points towards
words.exampleEn: in the direction of something, They headed in the direction of the village.

words.definitionEn: one of a group of senior managers who run a company
words.exampleEn: a non-executive director

words.definitionEn: if two people disagree or one person disagrees with another about something, they have a different opinion about it
words.exampleEn: Even friends disagree sometimes.

words.definitionEn: to become impossible to see
words.exampleEn: With that, Matt promptly disappeared.

words.definitionEn: an unexpected event, such as a very bad accident, a flood or a fire, that kills a lot of people or causes a lot of damage
words.exampleEn: the world's worst humanitarian disaster

words.definitionEn: to be the first person to become aware that a particular place or thing exists
words.exampleEn: Cook is credited with discovering Hawaii.

words.definitionEn: an act or the process of finding somebody/something, or learning about something that was not known about before
words.exampleEn: Researchers in this field have made some important new discoveries.

words.definitionEn: a conversation about somebody/something; the process of discussing somebody/something
words.exampleEn: I discovered something interesting during a family discussion.

words.definitionEn: an illness affecting humans, animals or plants, often caused by infection
words.exampleEn: heart/lung/liver disease

words.definitionEn: the amount of space between two places or things
words.exampleEn: a short/long distance

words.definitionEn: a company that provides regular flights to take passengers and goods to different places
words.exampleEn: an airline pilot/passenger

words.definitionEn: no longer married because your marriage has been legally ended
words.exampleEn: My parents are divorced.

words.definitionEn: an official paper, book or electronic file that gives information about something, or that can be used as evidence or proof of something
words.exampleEn: Please read and sign the attached document.

words.definitionEn: twice as much or as many as usual
words.exampleEn: a double helping

words.definitionEn: twice as much or as many as
words.exampleEn: His income is double hers.

words.definitionEn: a number or amount that is twice as much or as many as another number or amount
words.exampleEn: He gets paid double for doing the same job I do.

words.definitionEn: to become, or make something become, twice as much or as many
words.exampleEn: Membership almost doubled in two years.

words.definitionEn: data that is downloaded from another computer system
words.exampleEn: A staggering 99.8 % of digital music downloads are to mobile handsets.

words.definitionEn: to get data from another computer, usually using the internet
words.exampleEn: download something, to download files/music/software

words.definitionEn: on a floor of a house or building lower than the one you are on, especially the one at ground level
words.exampleEn: a downstairs bathroom

words.definitionEn: a play for the theatre, television or radio
words.exampleEn: a costume/period/courtroom/crime drama

words.definitionEn: a picture made using a pencil or pen rather than paint
words.exampleEn: a pencil/pen-and-ink/charcoal drawing

words.definitionEn: a series of images, events and feelings that happen in your mind while you are asleep
words.exampleEn: I had a really weird dream last night.

words.definitionEn: to experience a series of images, events and feelings in your mind while you are asleep
words.exampleEn: Did I talk in my sleep? I must have been dreaming.

words.definitionEn: a journey in a car or other vehicle
words.exampleEn: Let's go for a drive.

words.definitionEn: the way that somebody drives a vehicle; the act of driving
words.exampleEn: She was convicted of dangerous driving.

words.definitionEn: to allow something to fall by accident; to fall by accident
words.exampleEn: drop something, Be careful not to drop that plate.

words.definitionEn: an illegal substance that some people smoke, inject, etc. for the physical and mental effects it has
words.exampleEn: He does not smoke or take drugs.

words.definitionEn: not wet or sticky; without water or moisture
words.exampleEn: Is my shirt dry yet?

words.definitionEn: to become dry; to make something dry
words.exampleEn: He did the laundry and hung it out to dry.

words.definitionEn: to get money for work that you do
words.exampleEn: earn (something), He earns about $40 000 a year.

words.definitionEn: the world; the planet that we live on
words.exampleEn: the planet Earth

words.definitionEn: without problems or difficulty
words.exampleEn: The museum is easily accessible by car.

words.definitionEn: a process of teaching, training and learning, especially in schools, colleges or universities, to improve knowledge and develop skills
words.exampleEn: primary/elementary education

words.definitionEn: a change that somebody/something causes in somebody/something else; a result
words.exampleEn: the beneficial effects of exercise

words.definitionEn: used after negative phrases to state that a feeling or situation is similar to one already mentioned
words.exampleEn: Pete can't go and I can't either.

words.definitionEn: one or the other of two; it does not matter which
words.exampleEn: You can park on either side of the street.

words.definitionEn: one or the other of two; it does not matter which
words.exampleEn: You can park on either side of the street.

words.definitionEn: connected with electricity; using, produced by or producing electricity
words.exampleEn: an electric car/vehicle

words.definitionEn: connected with electricity; using or producing electricity
words.exampleEn: an electrical fault in the engine

words.definitionEn: a form of energy from charged elementary particles, usually supplied as electric current through cables, wires, etc. for lighting, heating, driving machines, etc.
words.exampleEn: materials that conduct electricity

words.definitionEn: having or using many small parts, such as microchips, that control and direct a small electric current
words.exampleEn: Airline passengers no longer have to turn off all electronic devices while on the plane.

words.definitionEn: living; not dead
words.exampleEn: Is your mother still alive?

words.definitionEn: completely
words.exampleEn: She was dressed all in white.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody a job to do for payment
words.exampleEn: employ somebody, How many people does the company employ?

words.definitionEn: a person who is paid to work for somebody
words.exampleEn: The firm has over 500 employees.

words.definitionEn: a person or company that pays people to work for them
words.exampleEn: They're very good employers (= they treat the people that work for them well).

words.definitionEn: with no people or things inside
words.exampleEn: an empty box/glass/bottle

words.definitionEn: the last part of a story, film, etc.
words.exampleEn: His stories usually have a happy ending.

words.definitionEn: acceptable; in an acceptable manner
words.exampleEn: Is the coffee all right?

words.definitionEn: the strength, effort and enthusiasm required for physical or mental activity, work, etc.
words.exampleEn: It's a waste of time and energy.

words.definitionEn: the part of a vehicle that produces power to make the vehicle move
words.exampleEn: a diesel/petrol engine

words.definitionEn: a person whose job involves designing and building engines, machines, roads, bridges, etc.
words.exampleEn: We worked with a team of scientists and engineers.

words.definitionEn: acceptable; in an acceptable manner
words.exampleEn: Is the coffee all right?

words.definitionEn: extremely large
words.exampleEn: an enormous house/dog

words.definitionEn: used to check that somebody agrees or understands
words.exampleEn: We've got to get up early, all right?

words.definitionEn: to come or go into something
words.exampleEn: Knock before you enter.

words.definitionEn: the natural world in which people, animals and plants live
words.exampleEn: The government should do more to protect the environment.

words.definitionEn: the things that are needed for a particular purpose or activity
words.exampleEn: a useful piece of equipment for the kitchen

words.definitionEn: a mistake, especially one that causes problems or affects the result of something
words.exampleEn: spelling/typographical/grammatical errors

words.definitionEn: more with one person, thing, etc. than with others, or more in particular circumstances than in others
words.exampleEn: Teenagers are very fashion conscious, especially girls.
